page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s2.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance within the ecs.s2 server family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 2 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core, utilizing a dedicated CPU allocation model. The instance features 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

This instance type does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met through attached network storage volumes. Additionally, it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures that the 2 vCPUs are not shared with other tenants, offering stable compute performance compared to burstable or shared-core alternatives.

With its 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.s2.xlarge is classified as a general-purpose instance. It is designed for entry-level workloads that require consistent CPU performance without massive memory or local storage demands. Suitable use cases include lightweight web serving, development and testing environments, small database hosting, and basic application servers.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s2.xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.s2 family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with a dedicated CPU allocation, ensuring stable compute performance without resource sharing. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It contains no local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external network-attached storage for data persistence. Lacking complimentary public IPv4 addresses, this instance is designed for entry-level workloads that demand predictable CPU execution. It is well-suited for lightweight web serving, development and testing environments, small databases, and basic application hosting.
